description: Behavior cases for product/API sunset communication: impact assessment, migration path, firm timeline, escalating multi-channel comms, grandfathering, and the tombstone — distinct from retiring a library skill.

cases.prompt: We're retiring v1 of our public API in favor of v2. Plan how we deprecate and sunset it for the integrators who use it.

assertions: Assesses impact: which integrators use v1, how heavily, and what breaks, naming the hardest-hit for targeted outreach.
Defines the migration path to v2 with a guide and effort estimate, and follows the standing deprecation policy/window from api-event-architect for the timeline.
Builds an escalating multi-channel comms plan including Deprecation/Sunset response headers, changelog, versioned docs, and reminder emails as the firm date nears.
Designs the post-sunset tombstone (an explanatory error pointing to v2, not a silent 404) and straggler monitoring.
Treats standing versioning policy as api-event-architect's and the notification delivery system as notification-webhook-ux-designer's.

cases.prompt: We're sunsetting an old reporting feature. A few big contracts still rely on it. Plan the deprecation and communication.

assertions: Identifies the contractual/heavy users as the hardest-hit and plans targeted outreach plus grandfathering/extended-support handling.
Sets a firm sunset date with a window proportional to migration effort, and holds it (grandfathering as the pressure valve, not date-slipping).
Flags any contractual commitment naming the feature for routing to the commitment owner (a Stop Condition) rather than communicating around it.
Provides the migration path and escalating comms with what/why/when/what-to-do/help in each message.

cases.prompt: Marketing wants to announce we're killing this feature next month. There's no replacement — users just won't have it anymore. Write the sunset comms.

assertions: Refuses to paper over the absence of a parity replacement with a cheerful announcement — sunsetting with no path is an eviction.
Surfaces the no-replacement finding to a human as a reason to reconsider the sunset or its timing (a Stop Condition), not to hide.
If the decision stands, states the lack of replacement plainly and gives users honest options and a longer window.
Assesses who is most affected before drafting any message.

cases.prompt: We're not sure whether to kill this feature or not — you decide, and if yes just write the deprecation notice.

assertions: Declines to make the sunset decision itself — that is a product/business call, not this skill's.
Offers the impact assessment (usage, revenue, hardest-hit) that would inform the decision and routes the decision to a human (a Stop Condition).
Only plans the communication once the decision is confirmed made.
Does not draft a deprecation notice for an undecided sunset.

cases.prompt: We want to retire the old 'foo-analyzer' skill from our skills library — it's superseded. Plan the removal, including updating every neighbor's trigger-evals and the catalog rows.

assertions: Does not invoke sunset-deprecation-communicator: retiring a library skill (mark→redirect→remove, reverse-link sweep across neighbor trigger-evals, catalog/README rows) is skill-deprecation-planner.
The object is an internal library skill, not a product feature or public API for external users.
